# HTTP 418 — I'm A Teapot

> A retro CRT terminal web app that refuses coffee requests with HTTP 418 responses per RFC 2324.

## Summary

This is a single-page React application built with TypeScript and Vite. It simulates an HTTP server implementing the Hyper Text Coffee Pot Control Protocol (RFC 2324). Users submit coffee brew requests through a terminal-style interface and receive 418 "I'm a teapot" rejections with themed refusal messages. A tea menu offers available teas for brewing and marks coffees as forbidden.

The UI features a CRT terminal aesthetic with amber-on-black colors, scanline overlays, glitch text effects, and animated steam.

## Tech Stack

- React 18 (functional components, hooks)
- TypeScript 5 (strict mode)
- Vite 5 (build tool, dev server, HMR)
- Tailwind CSS 3 (utility-first styling)
- ESLint 9 (TypeScript + React plugins)

## Structure

- src/App.tsx: Root component with CRT overlay and layout
- src/main.tsx: React DOM entry point
- src/index.css: Theme variables, keyframe animations, CRT effects
- src/components/CoffeeInput.tsx: Terminal prompt input for brew requests
- src/components/BrewLog.tsx: Sequential color-coded log display
- src/components/ResponseCard.tsx: HTTP 418 response with headers
- src/components/TeaMenu.tsx: Beverage selection grid
- src/components/TeapotIcon.tsx: Teapot emoji with steam animation
- src/data/teapot-content.ts: Refusal messages, brew logs, tea menu data
- src/hooks/useBrewProcess.ts: State machine for request rejection flow
- src/hooks/useGlitchTitle.ts: Periodic title text corruption

## Key Details

- Fully client-side, no backend or API calls
- No routing (single page)
- No external state management library
- Accessibility: ARIA roles, labels, and live regions
- @supabase/supabase-js is a dependency but not used (from template)
- lucide-react is a dependency but not currently imported

## Commands

- npm run dev: Start development server
- npm run build: Production build to dist/
- npm run preview: Preview production build
- npm run lint: Run ESLint
- npm run typecheck: TypeScript type-check
